title: UPS Haslet AGVs stopped moving; Optisweep service restored operations after restart
retrieval_text: Case 00229374 at UPS Fort Worth, TX (Haslet) reported that AGVs were not moving and nothing was feeding the tippers during preload. Site reported robots appeared to have tasks and totes but stopped before tipper lines, could not remove product at the hospital area, and RMS showed no robot errors. Remote troubleshooting was delayed by UPS-side Zscaler/VPN access issues. Site-side checks included operator station status, AVEVA review, pause/unpause attempts, and heartbeat checks, with Tipper 1 reported at 0 while other tippers had nonzero heartbeat values. Once access to 10.27.80.165 was obtained through a field PC, support observed Ignition up, reviewed database timeout and OPC/heartbeat-related evidence, and Mitchel Flynn restarted Optisweep services. The site continued to run after the restart. Root cause was not explicitly validated in source evidence.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Optisweep service degradation or stalled service state affecting heartbeat/data flow between WCS, Ignition, and field systems
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Service restart restored operations; source notes state restart was prompted after finding missing heartbeats; multiple observations suggest data was not being passed properly while robots still had tasks.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Database query timeout activity in WCSWebApplication may have contributed to the incident
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: RCA evidence shows Event ID 10100 and 20102 at 4/4/2026 1:48:37 AM with execution timeout expired and failed DbCommand messages; later case notes reference database timeouts around 2 AM site time.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: OPC communications or heartbeat path disruption between Optisweep/Ignition and tipper or hospital interfaces
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Evidence includes missing heartbeat discussion, AVEVA showing tippers closed while field showed open, pause/unpause ineffective, and multiple OPC write errors / Bad at OPC comms messages in shared screenshots.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: UPS-side Zscaler/VPN outage impeded diagnosis but was not proven to be the operational root cause
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Multiple Teams and case notes document inability to remote in due to Validation Pending 2 / Zscaler issues; however, site symptoms existed independently and operations were restored after service restart.

title: UPS TXRTH WCS/service failure with AGVs not moving, tote removal blocked, and suspected Ignition/OptiSweep service disruption
retrieval_text: Case 228086 candidate CAT-1 WCS/service failure at UPS TXRTH on 2026-02-20. Reported symptoms included 4-6 AGVs not moving in various areas after a heartbeat issue, inability to remove totes from hospital, question marks on the HMI, and suspected Ignition crash or OptiSweep service disruption. Troubleshooting included remote access, review of Ignition memory trend and uptime/performance pages, RMS and AGV status review, GetAgvStatuses API testing, and later restart-oriented guidance for Ignition and OptiSweep validation. Evidence does not validate root cause. Escalation and customer communication gaps were also documented, especially lack of a proper UPS bridge call.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Ignition gateway crash or instability may have contributed to the service disruption
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Multiple participants explicitly questioned whether Ignition crashed; memory trend and uptime/performance pages were reviewed; restart guidance for Ignition was later shared.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: OptiSweep service responsiveness issue may have contributed to AGV status/control disruption
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Source mentions building saying something about OptiSweep, later guidance says to verify service response using GetAgvStatuses and restart OptiSweep if no response is given.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Heartbeat-related communication issue may have caused AGVs to stop moving without explicit AGV faults
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Initial report states 4-6 bots not moving after heartbeat issue, while another message says there was no fault or failed AGVs and it happened during normal operation.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: A zone-level AGV/system state inconsistency may have existed
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Later message states system errors showed a zone missing an AGV; RMS/HMI screenshots also show .NET Control not created and system/AGV error areas.

title: UPS Fort Worth Haslet ACB hospital station unable to induct totes with AGV/tote state mismatch and recovery actions required
retrieval_text: Case 00223554 at UPS Fort Worth, TX (Haslet) involved the ACB hospital station being unable to induct totes. Source records show the case opened around 2:11-2:12 AM on 2025-09-26, a CBRE work order was entered at 1:35 AM CST, and a Teams bridge was posted at 3:05 AM. Salesforce updates state the original issue was resolved by 4:07 AM with the site able to add and remove totes, and all robots were re-added by 5:05 AM. Later Teams discussion on the evening of 2025-09-26 describes a related or recurring hospital/tipper disruption where totes could be removed but not added, AGVs 1542188 and 1555028 were removed, AGV 1555012 had a disconnected tote 51, and API dog/Aveva endpoints were used to induce tote movement and remove a stuck tote. Multiple messages suggest a candidate cause involving an AGV being removed without removing its associated tote, but root cause is not explicitly validated in the source.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Hospital station disablement due to AGV being removed without removing the associated tote
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Teams messages state this explanation was provided by Andy and Zane, and later troubleshooting messages repeatedly reference removed AGVs with associated tote mismatch as the likely cause.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Hospital induct issue compounded by disconnected tote state and AGVs not picking up tasks under the rack
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Ekram Nafis summary at 9:06 PM reports AGV 012 with disconnected tote in system, AGVs 188 and 028 under the rack not picking up tasks, and uncertainty on what caused that state.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Initial incident described as hospital station server/services issue requiring OptiSweep service reset and site restart
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: CBRE work order and Salesforce closure email describe AGV hospital station server issues and resolution details of resetting OptiSweep services and restarting Hospital Lane.

title: UPS Fort Worth (Haslet) AGV/tipper flow stoppage with heartbeat timeout indications; service restart restored operation
retrieval_text: Case 00229716 at UPS Fort Worth, TX (Haslet) reported that after startup everything was waiting, no path was showing, and nothing was coming to the tippers. During sort start, AGVs lined up to go to tippers and all three lines stopped. Operators could not remove a tote at the hospital. Tippers showed heartbeat timeout while remaining active and enabled, with no RMS faults noted. L2 remotely investigated, coordinated escalation in Teams and Salesforce, and under direction from Mitchel Flynn the team E-stopped the AGVs, restarted the Optisweep service, removed the E-stop, and may have paused/unpaused tippers. Antonio confirmed proper AGV operation and hospital function afterward. Ignition and Windows event logs were saved, and switch log follow-up was assigned. Root cause was not explicitly validated in the source.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Optisweep service degradation or hung service state affecting AGV-to-tipper flow and hospital tote removal
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Recovery followed E-stop and Optisweep service restart; Teams guidance explicitly selected Optisweep rather than Ignition OPC; symptoms included all three lines stopping and heartbeat timeout indications.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Tipper communication or heartbeat issue visible at the application layer
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Justin documented all tippers showing heartbeat timeout while status remained active and enabled, and screenshots referenced tipper alarms and heartbeat views.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Underlying network or switch-related issue contributing to heartbeat loss or service disruption
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Resolution notes and Teams follow-up requested UPS switch logs, indicating unresolved infrastructure questions remained after recovery.

title: UPS Haslet AGV movement stoppage with repeated station/OPC UA disconnect behavior; service restart restored production
retrieval_text: Case 00229488 at UPS Fort Worth, TX (Haslet) documents a repeat production-impacting incident where AGVs were not moving and operations referenced the same issue from Saturday case 00229374 / JIRA CSPO-9007. Early triage noted most tippers in run position except two, a heartbeat reported as flat at 550, and operators manually clearing areas to prevent service failures. The case was escalated to software support and internal escalation resources. Customer pressure emphasized restoring operations within 30 minutes while also obtaining root cause and timeline details. At approximately 9:27 AM on 4/8/2026, the Optisweep Windows service was restarted remotely, after which the site was reported operating and getting production. Later investigation referenced Ignition and visual alarm evidence showing hospital lane and GLD1/OPC UA connection resets, including 4/7 21:15 events with RIO Module Ethernet Failure, ClearLink module communication loss, VFD not detected alarms, and OPC UA connection reset behavior. Additional 4/8 and 4/9 evidence showed connection resets on 10.27.80.156 and 10.27.80.157, keep-alive failures, subscription transfer failures, reconnect/rebuild activity, and discussion of possible network degradation. Root cause was not validated in the source.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Network degradation or intermittent communications instability impacted OPC UA/station connectivity and downstream system behavior.
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Teams discussion at 4/8 5:54 PM states best guess was network degradation based on DB event logs showing timeouts, with assumed OPC communications impact; multiple connection reset and keep-alive failure screenshots support communications instability.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: GLD1 / hospital lane ethernet or controller-side communication fault around 4/7 21:15 contributed to recurring disconnect symptoms seen again on 4/8.
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Wesley Saunders update cites a connection fault on GLD1 at 21:15 on 4/7 due to ethernet failure, aligned with visual alarm evidence showing RIO Module Ethernet Failure and ClearLink communication alarms plus OPC UA reset on 10.27.80.248.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Optisweep service state or subscription handling degraded after communications faults, and restart temporarily restored operations.
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Restart of Optisweep Windows service restored production at 9:27-9:30 AM; later logs show reconnecting sessions, subscription transfer failures, and monitored item rebuild activity. Recovery after restart is not proof of root cause.

raw_terms: hospital lane connection was reset multiple times today

title: UPS Haslet OptiSweep/AGV startup stoppage with no paths to tippers; tipper heartbeat failure observed and service restart restored flow
retrieval_text: Case 00229777 at UPS Fort Worth, TX (Haslet) describes an OptiSweep/AGV system startup issue where everything was waiting, no path was shown, and nothing was coming up to the tippers. Teams chat logged OptiSweep stopping around 5:25 AM on 2026-04-18. Kieran Hardy connected to the system, observed that the tipper heartbeat had failed similar to case 229716, and recovery actions included client placing the master e-stop on while OptiSweep was restarted and the tippers were paused and unpaused. The system was reported back up and running and the client agreed the case could be closed. Later Teams discussion on 2026-04-29 referenced network troubleshooting, disconnect logging, AGV-side logs, RMS/Athena logs, and a robot with frequent disconnect history, but these appear to be follow-on diagnostic context rather than the original outage event.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Tipper heartbeat failure may have caused the OptiSweep/AGV startup stoppage and lack of flow to tippers.
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Salesforce resolution details explicitly state the engineer saw that the tipper heartbeat had failed, and recovery followed restart plus tipper pause/unpause.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: The incident may be related to a recurring issue pattern similar to case 229716.
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Salesforce resolution notes state the heartbeat failure was the same as case 229716.

candidate_inferred_causes.cause_summary: Underlying network or AGV disconnect behavior may be a contributing factor, but this is not confirmed for the outage itself.
candidate_inferred_causes.basis: Later Teams discussion on 2026-04-29 references network troubleshooting, disconnect data, AGV-side logs, and a robot described as a frequent disconnect unit.
